summary: Comprehensive testing of partner signup overhaul: address validation, seeding, product currency, placeholder content, website settings, email templates, and favicon upload. Found and fixed a critical bug causing silent failure in partner_subscription/order creation. All 19 backend tests pass. Favicon upload UI verified in admin panel.

critical.issue: FIXED: UnboundLocalError: 'cannot access local variable timezone where it is not associated with a value'. Python treats 'timezone' as a local variable (due to 'from datetime import timezone' inside conditional blocks earlier in the function), causing UnboundLocalError when datetime.now(timezone.utc) is called in the free-trial-plan try/except block. This was silently swallowed by 'except Exception: pass', preventing partner_subscription and partner_order from being created. Fixed by removing redundant local imports at lines 902 and 911 in backend/routes/auth.py.

minor.issue: Does not include 'favicon_url' field in response when no favicon has been uploaded (field not in MongoDB document). GET /api/settings/public always includes it. Frontend handles this gracefully with fallback. Consider normalizing the response to always include favicon_url.

issues: Minor: When no favicon has been uploaded, the dropzone shows correctly. When a favicon is uploaded, Replace/Remove buttons appear — this is correct behavior.

action_items: Consider normalizing GET /api/admin/settings to always include favicon_url field (even if null) for API consistency
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: auth.py lines 902-912: Local 'from datetime import timezone' imports inside if blocks caused UnboundLocalError in the free-trial plan creation code path. The try/except Exception:pass block silently swallowed the error, making it impossible to debug without detailed tracing.
The 'except Exception: pass' anti-pattern in verify_partner_email (lines 947-1013) should be replaced with proper error logging to prevent future silent failures.

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Favicon UI section visible and correctly positioned

seed_data_creation: Two test tenant partners created (TestOrg 1773447364, TestOrg 1773447662) for testing. These remain in the DB. Favicon uploaded to platform admin settings.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Critical bug was fixed in verify-partner-email endpoint (auth.py): local 'from datetime import timezone' imports inside conditional blocks caused UnboundLocalError when creating partner_subscription/order. All 19 tests pass after fix. Favicon upload UI is working and visible in Admin > Settings > Organization Info page. Test data: Two test tenants in DB (TestOrg 1773447364 and TestOrg 1773447662) - can be cleaned up. The platform admin settings now has a favicon_url set (base64 PNG).

rca_of_the_issue.bug: partner_subscription and partner_order not created after verify-partner-email
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Python function scoping issue: when 'from datetime import timezone' appears inside any conditional block within a function, Python marks 'timezone' as a LOCAL variable for the ENTIRE function scope. In verify_partner_email, two conditional blocks (lockout check and too-many-attempts check) each had 'from datetime import timezone'. In the normal success path (correct OTP, no lockout), neither block executes, so 'timezone' is never assigned. When the free-trial subscription creation code runs 'datetime.now(timezone.utc)', Python raises UnboundLocalError because it looks for the local 'timezone' variable which was never assigned. This exception is caught and discarded by 'except Exception: pass'.
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction: 1. Register a partner, 2. Verify with correct OTP on first attempt (no lockout) - subscription/order not created
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Removed the redundant 'from datetime import timezone' (and timedelta) imports inside the conditional blocks since these are already imported at the module level (lines 1-7 of auth.py)
files_changed: backend/routes/auth.py: removed local imports at lines 902 and 911
